arguments.task: Open official Lark developer documentation and collect evidence for these fields only from first-party pages: product/API description, documented authentication methods, API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL), rate limits, and whether there is official MCP support. Also find an official pricing or developer page that clarifies whether developers can self-serve API access or if partner/contact-sales approval is required. Prefer canonical documentation pages that load in a browser and can be cited directly. Return the final answer with page titles, canonical URLs visited, and concise extracted evidence snippets.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on official Lark pages to gather canonical evidence for structured research fields.

raw_result.description: Enterprise collaboration platform with messaging and productivity features plus developer APIs and app integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer site is available at open.larksuite.com, but the browser evidence gathered shows the documentation pages repeatedly failed to render in this environment (blank/SPA pages). Based on the official site structure and page path visited, OAuth-based app authorization and server-side app credential flows appear likely, but the exact complete set of documented auth methods could not be verified from readable page content in this pass.
raw_result.gating_reason: Could not verify a readable official pricing or access page in this pass. Because Lark developer access for enterprise workspaces commonly depends on workspace/app administration, this record is marked for human review rather than treated as self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Official open platform appears to provide broad platform APIs for messaging, docs, calendar, contacts, and admin/integration scenarios, but breadth could not be validated from rendered page text in this pass.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP page or server documentation was verified from readable first-party content in this pass.

raw_result.blocker: Official documentation pages on open.larksuite.com did not render reliably in this environment, preventing verification of the exact auth and access details needed for a confident toolkit build without manual human confirmation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit documentation could not be read because the official docs pages did not render successfully in this environment.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong official developer domain exists, but the browser task repeatedly encountered blank/JS-rendered documentation pages across multiple canonical doc URLs, indicating thin-or-JS-rendered-docs risk for automated verification.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields auth_methods, access_tier, api_surface.type breadth support, mcp_exists, and buildability_verdict could not all be supported by readable first-party page content because the official documentation pages failed to render in this environment.
